name: Action for Resilience, Climate and Health Collaborative at USC
mission: ARCH SC’s mission aligns with that of nonprofit ARCH Collaborative: to advance innovative, evidence‑based solutions that build community resilience against chronic disease and extreme weather. We believe that addressing chronic disease and climate‑driven disasters requires integrating public health, environmental justice and community empowerment. Our chapter will bring people together to design a
membership_benefits: Becoming a member of ARCH SC offers rich professional development and service opportunities. 501(c)3 non-profit, ARCH Collaborative, prides itself on fostering an inclusive culture and investing in our members’ development as future leaders in climate and health. As part of the USC student chapter of ARCH Collaborative, members will gain hands‑on experience through community engagement programs, s
contact: David Rios Palomares Mission ARCH SC’s mission aligns with that of nonprofit ARCH Collaborative: to advance innovative, evidence‑based solutions that build community resilience against chronic disease and extreme weather. We believe that addressing chronic disease and climate‑driven disasters requires integrating public health, environmental justice and community empowerment. Our chapter will bring people together to design and disseminate practical tools and educational programs. Some examples are teaching residents how to engage in policymaking and environmental justice, distributing supplies and resources to help them adapt to extreme weather, and mentoring the next generation of leaders through the ARCH Scholars Program. We are committed to grounding our work in rigorous research by examining links between environmental exposures and health outcomes and assessing the impact of our programs. Ultimately, our mission is to create healthier, more resilient communities by fostering collaboration, innovation and inclusion. Membership Benefits Becoming a member of ARCH SC offers rich professional development and service opportunities. 501(c)3 non-profit, ARCH Collaborative, prides itself on fostering an inclusive culture and investing in our members’ development as future leaders in climate and health. As part of the USC student chapter of ARCH Collaborative, members will gain hands‑on experience through community engagement programs, such as workshops that teach Los Angeles residents how to advance health and environmental justice, and the Community Climate Adaptation Program, which supplies resources to help residents (particularly unhoused individuals) prepare for extreme weather. Members can also participate in the ARCH Scholars Program, where they serve as mentors and train LA residents to design evidence‑based interventions for health and the environment. Research opportunities include analyzing links between environmental exposures and health outcomes and evaluating program impacts, allowing members to contribute to cutting‑edge knowledge while building practical skills. Overall, membership offers networking with dedicated professionals, leadership experience, and the chance to make a tangible difference in community resilience.

name: Advanced Degree Consulting Club at USC
mission: Advanced Degree Consulting Club (ADCC) serves advanced degree candidates (with a specific focus on PhD and Master students) that are passionate about pursuing management consulting as their career path.
membership_benefits: As a member of the Advanced Degree Consulting Club, you will be stepping into a vibrant community designed to accelerate your professional growth and development. This isn’t just a club—it’s a powerful network where your advanced academic background will be transformed into practical expertise. Here, you’ll sharpen your analytical and problem-solving skills through rigorous training sessions and h
contact: Riley Elmer , Email group officers Mission Advanced Degree Consulting Club (ADCC) serves advanced degree candidates (with a specific focus on PhD and Master students) that are passionate about pursuing management consulting as their career path. Membership Benefits As a member of the Advanced Degree Consulting Club, you will be stepping into a vibrant community designed to accelerate your professional growth and development. This isn’t just a club—it’s a powerful network where your advanced academic background will be transformed into practical expertise. Here, you’ll sharpen your analytical and problem-solving skills through rigorous training sessions and hands-on case studies, all while leveraging the deep knowledge and experience of fellow members who share your advanced academic journey. But the benefits go far beyond skill-building. You’ll gain exclusive access to a network of industry leaders from both the for-profit and nonprofit sectors—professionals who truly understand the challenges and opportunities of transitioning from academia to consulting. Through real-world consulting projects, you’ll apply your theoretical insights in ways that make a tangible impact, while frequent, constructive feedback from peers and mentors ensures your continuous personal and professional development. Additionally, the club’s robust resources—including industry reports, interview preparation sessions, and more—are strategically designed to help you navigate and excel in the consulting world. Joining the Advanced Degree Consulting Club isn’t just about enhancing your resume; it’s about embarking on a transformative journey that bridges the academic world with the fast-paced, dynamic field of consulting, setting you up for long-term success.

contact: Paris Esmailian , Email group officers Mission Alpha Gamma Alpha at USC is a cultural sorority committed to empowering our members through a deep connection to our Armenian heritage, fostering unity, and cultivating leadership. We strive to make a meaningful impact by engaging in philanthropic initiatives that support the local Los Angeles and Armenian communities. Through service, cultural preservation, and sisterhood, we aim to create a lasting legacy of positive change and personal growth for our members and the communities we serve. Membership Benefits Sisterhood and Lifelong Friendships: Connect with like-minded individuals who share your cultural background and values, fostering deep and lasting friendships. Diverse Events and Activities: Participate in a wide range of events, from social gatherings to philanthropic initiatives, providing opportunities for personal growth, leadership, and community engagement. Networking Opportunities: Meet sisters from other chapters and build a network of supportive peers and mentors, both within and beyond the sorority. Academic Support: Access resources and support systems designed to help you excel academically, including study groups, tutoring, and guidance from fellow members. Cultural Enrichment: Immerse yourself in Armenian culture through events, traditions, and activities that celebrate and preserve your heritage. Leadership Development: Take on leadership roles within the sorority, enhancing your skills in organization, event planning, and team management. Community Service: Engage in meaningful philanthropic work that gives back to the Los Angeles and Armenian communities, making a positive impact while fulfilling the sorority’s mission.

name: Association of Indian Students
mission: You bring with you everything that makes India so uniquely Indian: the rich culture, the poetic art, the aromatic food, and the engaging people. You bring with you the power of meditation, the Yoga and the Ayurveda, the Taj Mahal and the Gateway of Indiaa, Bollywood films and the Bachchans, the Ambassadors and AutoRickshaws, the IPL and the Tendulkar, the Akbar and the Birbal, the Sitar and the Ta
membership_benefits: Members can attend the meetings and participate in the events Lifetime membership
contact: Veer Vora , Arnav Rupesh Banthia , Email group officers Mission You bring with you everything that makes India so uniquely Indian: the rich culture, the poetic art, the aromatic food, and the engaging people. You bring with you the power of meditation, the Yoga and the Ayurveda, the Taj Mahal and the Gateway of Indiaa, Bollywood films and the Bachchans, the Ambassadors and AutoRickshaws, the IPL and the Tendulkar, the Akbar and the Birbal, the Sitar and the Tabla. You bring with you Raaga, Nritya, Natya, the politics, the weddings, one thousand dialects, 300 different ways of cooking the potato, and yet you bring with you the power of a single identity. We at Association of Indian Students indulge in everything Indian you bring, and locate amidst our diversities what we all have in common. We dance away to desi beats, splash colors on our faces, gulp those gol gappas off a saucer, sing the national anthem, and watch with bated breath as Dhoni smashes that last ball into the stands. And until the summons from home becomes too loud to ignore, we keep you connected to your roots. Come, let us recapture that thrill that runs through each of us when we think of home. Welcome to the Association of Indian Students at the University of Southern California. Membership Benefits Members can attend the meetings and participate in the events

name: Birnkrant Residential College
mission: Constructed in 1963, Birnkrant has been a part of USC over the last several decades. Created in honor of Cecele & Michael G. Birnkrant, the eight-story tower originally provided housing for 308 women. Over the many years of Birnkrant’s existence on the University Park campus, co-ed floors were created, and in 2011, the community began functioning as a first-year Honors community. First-year Presid
contact: Derek Murakami (he/him/his) Mission Constructed in 1963, Birnkrant has been a part of USC over the last several decades. Created in honor of Cecele & Michael G. Birnkrant, the eight-story tower originally provided housing for 308 women. Over the many years of Birnkrant’s existence on the University Park campus, co-ed floors were created, and in 2011, the community began functioning as a first-year Honors community. First-year Presidential, Trustee, & Mork Scholars live in Birnkrant’s tight-knit community after an application and interview process. Commonly referred to as “8 Floors of Open Doors,” the Birnkrant community is strong, as friendships and relationships are created that often span the course of the students’ time at USC. In 1994, Birnkrant began operating as a Residential College, with a full-time, live-in Faculty Master residing in the building and working to bridge the experiences inside and outside the classroom. The Faculty Master works alongside Residential Education staff to provide programs that introduce students to Los Angeles culture, art, food, and more. Traditionally, Birnkrant residents move in two days prior to the campus-wide move-in day, to attend a retreat with the community, faculty, and staff. This retreat often includes community building activities and an off-campus trip to the Hollywood Bowl for a performance by the LA Philharmonic. Residents take great pride in living in Birnkrant and often refer to it as one of their favorite memories and experiences as a USC student. Membership Benefits

contact: Email group officers Mission Brothers Breaking B.R.E.A.D. (BBB) is a pioneering student organization founded by black men at the University of Southern California, united by the principles of Brotherhood, Empowerment, Health, Leadership, Service, and Enrichment. Our mission, encapsulated in the acronym 'B.R.E.A.D.' — Barriers, Regrets, Egos, Animosity, and Doubt, is to break down these obstacles and cultivate a space where black men on campus can come together in brotherly fellowship. BBB serves as the nucleus of black male excellence, striving to empower and inspire our members to embrace leadership roles while redefining masculinity to address critical issues within the black community, including hyper-masculinity, misogynoir, and homophobia. Our organization warmly welcomes anyone who identifies as a black man, transcending all intersections within this identity. Our diverse membership enriches our learning experience, enabling individuals to advocate for themselves and each other. BBB is dedicated to fostering professional and leadership skills, while dismantling stereotypes and stigmas. Our ultimate aim is to become a permanent and transformative presence at the University of Southern California, leaving an enduring legacy of unity, empowerment, and positive change. Membership Benefits Becoming a member of Brothers Breaking B.R.E.A.D. (BBB) offers the chance to grow personally and professionally within a strong community of driven Black men. Members gain access to mentorship, networking, and professional development opportunities through partnerships with other organizations and companies BBB has connections to. The organization also provides leadership pathways whether through executive board positions, event planning, or community initiatives that help members build confidence and real-world skills. Beyond career growth, BBB fosters brotherhood, emotional support, and a safe space for open dialogue, creating a foundation for lifelong connections and collective empowerment.

name: Cale & Irani Residential Colleges
mission: Cale Residential College for Global Perspectives, engages students in the complexities of the world by immersing yourself in global exploration at USC. In this Residential College, you will build your intercultural competency and participate in critical dialogue regarding the ever changing global landscape. Take part in partnerships with academic departments and resource centers that focus on inte
contact: Dionely Acosta Mission Cale Residential College for Global Perspectives, engages students in the complexities of the world by immersing yourself in global exploration at USC. In this Residential College, you will build your intercultural competency and participate in critical dialogue regarding the ever changing global landscape. Take part in partnerships with academic departments and resource centers that focus on international affairs, business, arts/culture, media, and current events. Discuss critical issues regarding the world’s refugee crisis, hunger, poverty, socio-political strife, and global citizenry. Students will emerge from this Residential College Experience as global citizens ready to make a positive impact on the world. Irani Residential College for Performance Science focuses on the mind, body, and business. Students in this residential college will get to explore their untapped potential in learning how to break down barriers inhibiting leadership, decision-making, resilience, or creative potential. Students will attend programs and workshops that will help them understand brain and body function, human behavior, psychology, emotion, and leadership, all with the goals of maximizing performance, both individually and within an organization or community. Membership Benefits

name: Cardinal Gardens
mission: Cardinal Gardens is a popular apartment option among continuing undergraduate students. The community consists of nine individual townhouse-style buildings connected by winding, tree-lined walkways. Residents can choose between fully furnished one or two-bedroom apartments, each with a full kitchen, bathroom and a spacious, air-conditioned living room. Located on the north side of Jefferson Boulev
contact: Shamin;Mason Harrington;Mason Mission Cardinal Gardens is a popular apartment option among continuing undergraduate students. The community consists of nine individual townhouse-style buildings connected by winding, tree-lined walkways. Residents can choose between fully furnished one or two-bedroom apartments, each with a full kitchen, bathroom and a spacious, air-conditioned living room. Located on the north side of Jefferson Boulevard, Cardinal Gardens is across the street from campus and near to both the Lyon Recreation Center and Café 84. The newly renovated central courtyard provides a great gathering place for residents, where they can relax around the modern fountain in comfortable couches and chairs, grill on two gas barbecues, or spend a quiet evening sitting next to the large outdoor fireplace. Residents can also enjoy playing half-court basketball or lounging in the Customer Service Center, equipped with a 70 inch flat screen TV and pool and foosball tables. Membership Benefits

name: CURAM
mission: Our mission is to improve medical literacy in both the USC and greater LA metropolitan area. We aim to do this through a bi-annual magazine that explores pressing medical issues and the resources available to quail such problems. The magazine will be an undergraduate magazine that addresses medically relevant issues, education, research, and art. The magazine will comprise of four sections: 1) nov
membership_benefits: Membership is cohort-based, so one is able to benefit from a close knit community, amazing writing opportunities, and improving the multiple communities (e.g. South LA, USC, and Spanish speakers) through medical education. Likewise, there is always the prospect of cool merchandise. Lifetime membership
contact: Mugil Shanmugam , Sophia Hameed Mission Our mission is to improve medical literacy in both the USC and greater LA metropolitan area. We aim to do this through a bi-annual magazine that explores pressing medical issues and the resources available to quail such problems. The magazine will be an undergraduate magazine that addresses medically relevant issues, education, research, and art. The magazine will comprise of four sections: 1) novel medical research within the USC community (in the form of interviews, overviews of current USC research and published articles/journals), 2) explaining pertinent technology within the emerging medical field (this could be something as simple as "What is CRISPR" to explaining "How models of empathy are built and monitored"), 3) any artistic mediums that are inspired by medicine and its practice (poems, personal-essays, dance, etc.), 4) and lastly, the education portion, which would aim at elucidating any college-specific health related issues (mental health resources on campus, STD treatment on campus, updates on COVID-19 conditions on campus and the respective measures in place). Furthermore, the magazine will be translated into Spanish, so that we are able to reach LA's Spanish-speaking population. Membership Benefits Membership is cohort-based, so one is able to benefit from a close knit community, amazing writing opportunities, and improving the multiple communities (e.g. South LA, USC, and Spanish speakers) through medical education. Likewise, there is always the prospect of cool merchandise.

name: Dornsife Office of Overseas Studies

To continue to develop new international programs, and to increase the range of opportunities
contact: Andrea Storey Mission The Dana and David Dornsife College of Letters, Arts and Sciences of the University of Southern California expresses its commitment: To integrate study abroad into the undergraduate curriculum and to send a significant number of its undergraduates abroad for study at some time during their college lives. To continue to develop new international programs, and to increase the range of opportunities for study locations around the world. To maintain traditions of academic excellence in all study abroad programs. To encourage the appropriate foreign language preparation for study abroad. To facilitate both longer-term study (semester and year-long programs), as well as summer and other shorter-term programs, including student exchanges and other innovative possibilities for cultural exchange. To integrate study abroad with on-campus academic programs and activities; this includes effective advising, close coordination between international programs and relevant academic units, and development of relevant extracurricular programs. name: Emergency Medicine Student Interest Group
mission: The Emergency Medicine Student Interest Group (EMSIG), organized in 1995, has grown to become the largest student interest group at the Keck School of Medicine. EMSIG operates within the Department of Emergency Medicine at Los Angeles General Hospital under the direct guidance of Dr. Taku Taira, the organization’s advisor and faculty mentor. Through partnership with the Department of Emergency Med
contact: Email group officers Mission The Emergency Medicine Student Interest Group (EMSIG), organized in 1995, has grown to become the largest student interest group at the Keck School of Medicine. EMSIG operates within the Department of Emergency Medicine at Los Angeles General Hospital under the direct guidance of Dr. Taku Taira, the organization’s advisor and faculty mentor. Through partnership with the Department of Emergency Medicine, EMSIG offers medical students hands-on clinical experience, mentorship with residents and faculty members, procedure-based workshops, and research opportunities. EMSIG attracts a large number of first- and second-year medical students because its shadowing program allows students to gain early clinical experience in one of the country’s busiest and most prestigious emergency departments. Owing to the success of this shadowing program is the enthusiasm that the attending physicians and residents in L.A. General’s emergency department have for teaching. Faculty members from the Emergency Department also give monthly lunchtime lectures to introduce medical students to topics of interest ranging from head trauma to shark bites and lead workshops in techniques such as suturing and intubation. The Department of Emergency Medicine has been generous in offering mentorship through EMSIG in a variety of ways. Students may be paired up with a faculty mentor early in their medical school careers. EMSIG’s annual end-of-the-year reception also gives medical students the chance to talk with fourth-year-students who just completed the match process, residents, and faculty members from the Department of Emergency Medicine. Mentors are just as willing to help students navigate their course toward finding a specialty of interest as they are in sharing their own interest in Emergency Medicine. EMSIG also offers medical students the chance to attend several Emergency Medicine conferences. Regardless of which specialty medical students in EMSIG end up choosing, members of EMSIG credit the interest group in playing an important role in providing clinical experience in an exciting field and in helping them make personal career decisions. Membership Benefits

name: Flavors
mission: Flavors is a cultural club with the initiative to experience different cultures through taste, cuisine and conversation. Food is a key way of understanding a culture and an even better way to create meaningful relationships. We want to quite literally 'break bread' with our peers and engage with our student body through a shared enthusiasm and curiosity of our respective cuisine and cultures. Tryi
contact: Ryan Hsu , Ola Kalu , Selin Demircan , Emma Massey , Email group officers Mission Flavors is a cultural club with the initiative to experience different cultures through taste, cuisine and conversation. Food is a key way of understanding a culture and an even better way to create meaningful relationships. We want to quite literally 'break bread' with our peers and engage with our student body through a shared enthusiasm and curiosity of our respective cuisine and cultures. Trying different or new foods can be a whole adventure, it can be daunting yet very exciting. We want to encourage people to open their minds to trying new things because after all you never know what you'll really like until you try it. Our main initiative is to spark conversation, in a world that nowadays is so divided and defined by labels, we wanted to create an open atmosphere where people can transcend cultural barriers and truly get to know one another. We want to break the ice to initiate a more engaging dialogue where people from all walks of life can come together to share and discuss their cultures, values, passions and just have a good conversation. Membership Benefits

contact: Alashia Jones Mission The mission of GAASA is to build community, strengthen identity, and support the academic, cultural, and personal growth of generational African American students at USC. We aim to create a space for connection, education, and empowerment while welcoming all students who wish to engage, learn, and support our community. Through programming, dialogue, advocacy, and service, GAASA works to honor our history, uplift our members, and contribute positively to the USC campus. Membership Benefits Members of GAASA gain access to a supportive community centered on the experiences and history of generational African American students. The organization provides opportunities for leadership development, peer mentorship, cultural education, and open dialogue about identity, advocacy, and campus life. Members also benefit from workshops, community-building events, volunteer opportunities, and collaborations with campus partners. GAASA offers a space to connect, learn, and actively contribute to strengthening the presence and wellbeing of generational African American students at USC, while welcoming all students who wish to engage, learn, and support the mission.

name: Global Medical Brigades
mission: Our goal is to permanently end rural poverty through new medical and economic developments that ensure long lasting impact. As an organization, we strive to provide students with hands-on volunteering and shadowing opportunities to bring attention to public health disparities and improve worldwide health care sustainability. During break, our organization travels to rural communities of either Gua
membership_benefits: Members have the opportunity to partake in an international movement to implement sustainable healthcare systems and increase access to quality healthcare. Members will be trained in healthcare related topics, cultural competency, and global health issues to equip them to make a difference in any healthcare or clinical setting they may find themselves in. Members will have access to networking opp
contact: Amanda Lopez , Averi Nguyen , Cecilia Tai , Email group officers Group Not Registered Yet Mission Our goal is to permanently end rural poverty through new medical and economic developments that ensure long lasting impact. As an organization, we strive to provide students with hands-on volunteering and shadowing opportunities to bring attention to public health disparities and improve worldwide health care sustainability. During break, our organization travels to rural communities of either Guatemala, Honduras, Belize, Greece, Panama, and Ghana to volunteer among medical professionals to provide essential medical services. During the semester, we hold medical workshops, fundraisers, general body meetings, volunteering events, guest speakers, and socials to build our GMB community on campus. Membership Benefits Members have the opportunity to partake in an international movement to implement sustainable healthcare systems and increase access to quality healthcare. Members will be trained in healthcare related topics, cultural competency, and global health issues to equip them to make a difference in any healthcare or clinical setting they may find themselves in. Members will have access to networking opportunities with similarly passionate health equity advocates, pre-health students, pre-professional students and healthcare professionals. They will also have access to pre-health skills workshops, keynote presentations with healthcare professionals and grad student guest speakers. Members will have access to volunteer opportunities that utilize the DEI, pre-health, and cultural competency training they receive to impact our local community. Furthermore, members will have the opportunity to apply to partake in our annual medical brigades to Global Brigades free health clinics in partner countries, such as Guatemala, Honduras, Belize, Greece, Panama and Ghana.

membership_benefits: Members of Global Pharmacy Association (GlobalRx) will have the opportunity to engage in Global Pharmacy Experiences, where informational meetings will be held to educate members on medications available outside of the U.S. as well as how the world of pharmacy operates. Members will have the opportunity to participate in meetings with large organizations from around the world, which will provide n
contact: Romina Hacopian Mission The Global Pharmacy Association (GlobalRx) is dedicated to international collaboration and services through the advancement of pharmacy practices. Our mission is to advance our own knowledge of pharmaceutical practices around the world. Through our travels, we aim to improve health outcomes, make a positive impact on public health, build cross-cultural connections, and create a global network of pharmacy professionals who will improve global healthcare. With these experiences, we hope to inspire the next generation of pharmacists to become culturally competent and passionate leaders of global healthcare. Membership Benefits Members of Global Pharmacy Association (GlobalRx) will have the opportunity to engage in Global Pharmacy Experiences, where informational meetings will be held to educate members on medications available outside of the U.S. as well as how the world of pharmacy operates. Members will have the opportunity to participate in meetings with large organizations from around the world, which will provide networking benefits. Global networking and cultural exchange will benefit members on their level of knowledge and broadening their perspective of pharmacy. Members will be part of an initiative to improve global healthcare around the world and advocate for public health policies.

name: Graduate and Family Housing
mission: Graduate and Family Housing is endearingly referred to as GradFam. USC offers approximately 2,000 spaces for graduate students and students with families. Since the housing needs of graduate students and families typically differ from those of undergraduate students, Graduate and Family Housing is located in the smaller buildings in the North University Park area. Graduate student housing offers a
contact: Hector Solis-Ortiz Mission Graduate and Family Housing is endearingly referred to as GradFam. USC offers approximately 2,000 spaces for graduate students and students with families. Since the housing needs of graduate students and families typically differ from those of undergraduate students, Graduate and Family Housing is located in the smaller buildings in the North University Park area. Graduate student housing offers a variety of options from single occupancy studios and apartments that allow students to live and study on their own to the lower cost option of shared bedrooms and apartments units. Family Housing mainly consists of one-bedroom apartments, but there are a few two-bedroom apartments for those families that require a little more space. There are several amenities within the graduate and family communities including lounge spaces, gyms, courtyards, and play areas. We offer live in staff including Resident Assistants, a Residential Community Coordinator, and a Faculty in Residence who put on events, provide nightly emergency assistance, and support resident development. Membership Benefits

contact: Brian Head , Email group officers Mission Graduate Association of Music Students (GAMS) is dedicated to fostering a vibrant, inclusive community by creating meaningful performance opportunities that celebrate our diverse student body. GAMS further benefits USC Thornton students by providing performance opportunities which would otherwise not exist, particularly across departmental and divisional boundaries. 3.	GAMS also invites distinguished musicians and artists from around the world to share their knowledge with the USC musical community. 4.	As well, GAMS supports student well-being by hosting guest lectures and wellness sessions in partnership with the Musicians' Health and Wellness Committee, ensuring a holistic, nurturing environment for all members. contact: Email group officers Mission HackSC is a hackathon with a mission. Located in the heart of sunny Los Angeles, we provide our participants with a weekend full of innovation, connection, and education. In an interdisciplinary endeavor, over 800 hackers, designers, and visionaries engage in workshops, listen to speakers, and develop programs, tools, and build relationships that last for life. Every year, we recruit software developers, designers, marketing/PR coordinators, influencers, finance planners, photographers/videographers, outreach specialists, and so many other positions to join our organizing team. Team members select a primary team to support, and are encouraged to work on other teams' projects that they feel strongly about and that will help develop their skills. If you’re passionate and eager to learn, we want you on our team! Interested? Check out our website at https://hacksc.com for more info! We hope to see you soon! Membership Benefits

contact: Email group officers Mission The Health Behavior Research Student Association (HBRSA) at the University of Southern California is dedicated to empowering and supporting Ph.D. students in Health Behavior Research. Our mission is to promote academic excellence, professional growth, and community engagement by serving as the collective voice of our student body to faculty and program administration. Through organized academic, networking, and career development initiatives, HBRSA fosters opportunities for intellectual and professional advancement. Additionally, we aim to build a vibrant and supportive community by facilitating social and volunteer activities, enriching student life and creating lasting connections among peers. HBRSA is committed to enhancing the graduate experience, advancing the Health Behavior Research field, and nurturing the success of all HBR students at USC. Membership Benefits As a member of HBRSA, you gain access to a wealth of academic, professional, and networking resources tailored to support your growth in Health Behavior Research Ph.D. program. Members enjoy exclusive career development events, skill-building workshops, and opportunities to connect with faculty and industry professionals. Beyond academic and professional advantages, HBRSA fosters a supportive community through social events and volunteer activities that help build lasting friendships and connections. By joining, you’ll be part of a network that actively enhances the graduate student experience and advocates for the needs and success of all Health Behavior Research students at USC. All USC students, staff, faculty, and alumni are eligible to be members. Open membership allows individuals outside of the Health Behavior Research Ph.D. program to engage with our community and contribute to our mission of fostering academic, professional, and social development.

name: Human Rights Student Interest Group
mission: The Human Right Student Interest Group works with the Keck Human Rights Clinic to provide forensic evaluation and documentation to any immigrant or displaced individual residing in the United States who requires documentation of maltreatment in order for justice to be carried out. Our mission is 3-fold: 1. Justice: Members of the Human Rights Student Interest Group advocate on behalf of asylum see
contact: Email group officers Mission The Human Right Student Interest Group works with the Keck Human Rights Clinic to provide forensic evaluation and documentation to any immigrant or displaced individual residing in the United States who requires documentation of maltreatment in order for justice to be carried out. Our mission is 3-fold: 1. Justice: Members of the Human Rights Student Interest Group advocate on behalf of asylum seekers by providing pro bono forensic medical and psychological evaluations to corroborate claims of maltreatment. 2. Education: Medical students are given the opportunity to assist with forensic evaluations and report drafting, allowing students to gain experience as advocates for health and human rights. Medical students also learn about global issues and cultural competence through assisting with these examinations. 3. Training: The USC Human Rights Student Interest Group hosts nationwide training sessions for physicians to gain the skills necessary to perform forensic evaluations and register under PHR's umbrella of providers. Membership Benefits

La CASA strives to educate the campus about Latinx identity and the culture represented within the community (i.e. Central & South America, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Mexico and Puerto Rico), by of
contact: La Casa , Email group officers Mission Established in 1972, La CASA provides empowerment through cultural identity, leadership and social consciousness development, as well as community building for undergraduate and graduate students at USC. La CASA strives to educate the campus about Latinx identity and the culture represented within the community (i.e. Central & South America, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Mexico and Puerto Rico), by offering programs and services that focus on intersectionality. contact: Email group officers Mission Liberty in North Korea at USC is a university chapter of the international NGO Liberty in North Korea. Our purpose is to advocate for and spread awareness of the issues pertaining to North Korean refugees, the Korean peninsula, and human rights. We have a responsibility to support and assist LiNK HQ by engaging in educational activities and fundraising. LiNK at USC hopes to support refugee populations by shifting the focus of our activism to the real history, politics, and conflicts that have led to North Korea’s present-day situation through an unfiltered and critical perspective. LiNK at USC strives to cooperate with other on- and off-campus organizations to foster mutual solidarity and raise awareness of the interconnectedness of North Korean refugees’ issues with those of other groups. membership_benefits: 1. Regular performance and writing opportunities 2. A community of fellow comedic artists 3. A greater platform to promote social justice issues personal to any given member 4. contact: Helen Culpepper Mission Ludus Remedium is USC’s first freeform, activism-focused comedy troupe that includes everything from improv to sketch to sock puppets to supporting student mental health campus-wide! Wait, what was that last one? For those of you who took Latin in High School and nabbed those extra points on the SAT, you might notice that Ludus means “play” or “prank” and remedium roughly translates to “cure” or “remedy.” Our mission statement is: To cure the USC community of anxiety, intolerance, injustice (and maybe indigestion) through comedy. Completing this statement could be as simple as making a stressed out student laugh at one of our weekly shows or as complex as increasing visibility on a marginalized issue through our monthly comedy through social justice activities (we make no promises on the matter of indigestion). We aim to build confidence and bust bigotry through healthy, humorous releases. Ludus has built an impressive array of specific games, events, and traditions to make that happen. Membership Benefits 1. Regular performance and writing opportunities 2. A community of fellow comedic artists 3. A greater platform to promote social justice issues personal to any given member 4. The chance to make a difference in the wider USC community using the performing arts

Students living in McCarthy Honors Residential College will have direct contact with Faculty in Residence as well as faculty, advisors, and experts across disciplines. Unique experiences in this Residential College will include connecting students to research opportunities and an early move-in summit focusing on community building and the holistic development of high achieving students. name: McMorrow, Nemirovsky & Bohnett Residential Colleges
mission: McMorrow Residential College for Innovation & Design aspires to support the interests and passions of students in the areas of technology, science, and design. The Priam Residential College for Innovation & Design welcomes all students, across academic disciplines, to connect an
contact: Andrea Valenzuela Mission McMorrow Residential College for Innovation & Design aspires to support the interests and passions of students in the areas of technology, science, and design. This community will encourage the pursuit of solutions to global challenges through creativity, innovation, and novelty. The Priam Residential College for Innovation & Design welcomes all students, across academic disciplines, to connect and create opportunities to transcend their academics and bring ideas to life. Nemirovsky Residential College for Sustainability, USC’s sustainability community, is an interdisciplinary society of students who are dedicated to building awareness around the principles of environmental, social, and economic sustainability through green living practices, educational activities, and societal engagement. Members vow to live a lifestyle that attempts to reduce their individual use of natural resources and lessen their impact on the greater earth, while building their own knowledge and leadership capabilities to address the challenges facing our communities across local and global scales. Bohnett Residential College is a community built around a core ideal of social action through entrepreneurship. In collaboration with faculty, staff, and social entrepreneurs within the Los Angeles community and beyond, students will engage with and seek innovative solutions for a broad range of social issues such as homelessness, gun violence, diversity & inclusion, college access, and many other pressing social justice concerns facing the world today. Through speaker series, small group seminars, professional and community collaborations, and the support of the Bohnett community investment fund, students will be given entrepreneurial skills and tools to innovate new concepts and technologies to address today’s most significant social challenges. Membership Benefits

Model United Nations of Southern California was formed in 2008 to meet this need. Since then, MUNSC has grown to be an organization involving nearly 100 students from a wide variety of backgrounds and one that is ranked
membership_benefits: As a member of the Model UN team, you’ll gain valuable skills in public speaking, negotiation, and diplomacy. You’ll have the opportunity to engage in debates on global issues, improving your research and critical thinking abilities. Participation in conferences allows you to collaborate with peers from different schools and backgrounds, expanding your network. You’ll also develop leadership and t
contact: Sabeeh Mirza , Email group officers Mission It all started when a group of like-minded students felt the need for a community where the world's most pressing issues could be discussed, debated, and (theoretically) resolved. Model United Nations of Southern California was formed in 2008 to meet this need. Since then, MUNSC has grown to be an organization involving nearly 100 students from a wide variety of backgrounds and one that is ranked #15 in the nation. Our team travels all around North America to compete in conferences with other universities, as well as hosts two of our own conferences: the prestigious SCMUN for middle and high school students, and our intercollegiate TrojanMUN, ranked the top Fall conference on the West Coast by Best Delegate. Model UN not only gives newfound knowledge on global affairs, but it improves critical thinking, writing, communication, public speaking, and diplomacy skills. Aside from traveling to conferences, MUNSC members are instantly welcomed into the family with events like bonding retreats, mixers, and social events. MUNSC is filled with some of the most innovative, intelligent, and passionate students you'll ever meet - it's a choice you will never regret. Membership Benefits As a member of the Model UN team, you’ll gain valuable skills in public speaking, negotiation, and diplomacy. You’ll have the opportunity to engage in debates on global issues, improving your research and critical thinking abilities. Participation in conferences allows you to collaborate with peers from different schools and backgrounds, expanding your network. You’ll also develop leadership and teamwork skills, which are beneficial in both academic and professional settings.

contact: Email group officers Mission The mission of NPCA at USC is to encourage, foster, and recognize an interest in community pharmacy ownership and entrepreneurship among future leaders of the profession. We are excited and proud to offer many opportunities for students to explore the many career paths within community pharmacy, such as a Community and Ambulatory Care Residency Panel, APO Wine & Cheese Night, Legislative Day, a Compounding Workshop, Health Fairs, and more. The purpose of the National Community Pharmacy Association at the University of Southern California (NCPA at USC) student chapter is to provide a forum for pharmacy students to learn about the many career opportunities available in community pharmacy practice. We believe that community pharmacy is at the cutting edge of exciting areas in pharmacy such as specialty drugs, ambulatory care, disease state management, medication therapy management, travel medicine, compounding, and entrepreneurship. It is our mission to prepare pharmacy students to become professionals who provide and promote exceptional pharmaceutical care in an independent practice setting, while promoting the growth and prosperity of independent community pharmacy practice in the United States. name: New North Residential College
mission: New North Residential College, originally called North Residential College, was founded in 1993 and consisted of EVK, College, University, and Harris Halls. contact: Jake Lafleur Mission New North Residential College, originally called North Residential College, was founded in 1993 and consisted of EVK, College, University, and Harris Halls. In 1997, New Residential College (consisting of just EVK and College Halls) was split off, and for the following 18 years, New and North were separate, each with their own Faculty Masters and programming. Past Faculty Masters and residents included Professor Florence Clark, Stanley Rosen, Stephen Toulmin, Cynthia Hendricks and Tim Gustafson. Past Faculty Masters of New North Residential College are Professors Phil Ethington and Ange-Marie Hancock. Professor Ethington began as Faculty Master of New in 2011, and Professor Hancock began as Faculty master of North in 2012. Considering that the student residents of New and North shared a common identity and common spaces, Ethington and Hancock decided to re-merge the four Halls once again but retain the historic dual name, which students had long ago adopted: “New North.” New North Residential College houses predominantly first-year residents and strives to integrate a dynamic and active social setting with high academic achievement. New North Residential College is in a premium location on campus- you can grab coffee at the Starbucks at Trojan Grounds on your three-minute walk to Leavey Library, toss a Frisbee on McCarthy Quad, and appreciate the view of the neighboring reflecting pool. Across the street is the Galen Center and the historic Shrine Auditorium. New North also benefits from having the EVK dining hall attached to their building, so residents connect with a diverse group of students from all over campus. Membership Benefits

contact: Email group officers Mission WE PROVIDE SPACE TO GATHER The Trojan Family is as diverse in faith, belief and philosophy of life as it is in other regards. name: Parkside Arts & Humanities Residential College
mission: On August 15, 2007, after nearly two years of construction at the cost $58 million, USC celebrated the opening of the Parkside Arts and Humanities Residential College (PRB) with the marching band, balloons and banners. “Student residences aren’t just places to sleep anymore,” said Michael L. Jackson, Vice Provost of Student Affairs, at the ceremony. “They’re part of the educational program.” Preci
contact: Marie Montoya Mission On August 15, 2007, after nearly two years of construction at the cost $58 million, USC celebrated the opening of the Parkside Arts and Humanities Residential College (PRB) with the marching band, balloons and banners. “Student residences aren’t just places to sleep anymore,” said Michael L. Jackson, Vice Provost of Student Affairs, at the ceremony. “They’re part of the educational program.” Precisely for these reasons, PRB was designed to create a space where students and faculty can form a strong scholarly community centered on the arts and humanities. Although intended for students of all disciplines, its theme allows for special interest floors dedicated to music, the visual arts, poetry and architecture. Living in Parkside Arts & Humanities Residential College is not limited to majors in the arts and humanities. Everyone who appreciates the role of culture and the exploration of ideas is welcome. Standing four stories tall and bordered by Exposition Boulevard, Watt Way and 37th Place, PRB is at the southwestern end of the University Park Campus, across from the Los Angeles Natural History Museum. Joining its two sister structures, Parkside International Residential College (IRC) and Parkside Apartments, to complete the Parkside Community. Residents live in suite-style apartments, amidst state-of-the-art study lounges, music study rooms, recreation room with ping-pong, foosball and air hockey tables, and a Performance Café, all designed in response to student requests for more shared space. Outside, PRB boasts its own green space, quad, community garden and outdoor gym workout station. PRB is also a few steps away from the Parkside Restaurant, known across campus for the high quality of its food. Membership Benefits

name: Parkside International Residential College
mission: Parkside International Residential College (IRC) opened in 2001, the brainchild of USC President Steven B. Sample and V.P. of Student Affairs Michael Jackson. At 295,000 square feet and four floors, it was given its theme in order to create dialogue on global issues, and to bring together international and domestic residents in a single vibrant community. Located in the lower left quadrant of the
contact: Estrella Cruz Mission Parkside International Residential College (IRC) opened in 2001, the brainchild of USC President Steven B. Sample and V.P. of Student Affairs Michael Jackson. At 295,000 square feet and four floors, it was given its theme in order to create dialogue on global issues, and to bring together international and domestic residents in a single vibrant community. Located in the lower left quadrant of the university park campus, bordered by Vermont Avenue, Exposition Boulevard, Watt Way and 37th Place, IRC provides generous suite-style living, a high quality restaurant downstairs, laundry and rec room on site, ample study space and lounge space, music practice rooms, performance café, classroom space, and an unmatched outdoor green space including fountain, fire pit, and community garden. Special Interest Floors in IRC include the Women in Science and Engineering (WiSE) Floor. While IRC is home to predominantly first-years students, Parkside Apartments (PKS) houses primarily students in their junior and senior years. Parkside Apartments is comprised of two adjoining six-story wings of two-person apartments, all with individual kitchenettes and bathrooms, offering a more independent living environment. Renovated in 2005, it was originally built to coincide with the 1984 Olympics and was part of the athletes’ Olympic Village. PKS also has an ongoing commitment to sustainability through recycling programs and the creation of the Michael Waterman Community Garden. Living at Parkside allows residents to participate in intellectual, cultural and social exchanges with people who have a wide range of backgrounds and interests, in one of the most attractive locations on campus. The physical beauty, cultural richness, and wealth of study opportunities outside the classroom make the Parkside community at USC special. They are a unique collaborative learning center, and a place where Trojans are proud to live. Membership Benefits

mission: Our mission is to provide a nurturing and empowering community for Womxn of Color who aspire to pursue a career in the legal field. membership_benefits: ❥ Mentorship Program: Connect with established fellow pre-law students, alumni, law professionals, or law students for personalized guidance on law school admissions, career planning, and navigating the legal field. ❥ Networking Opportunities: Attend panels, mixers, and events featuring successful womxn of color in law, offering valuable insights and connections for your legal career. ❥ Academic S
contact: Laisha Campos Mission Our mission is to provide a nurturing and empowering community for Womxn of Color who aspire to pursue a career in the legal field. As a pre-law organization aligned with USC Gould WOCC, our aim is to create a supportive space where members can embark on their journey towards law school admissions and navigate the complex legal landscape beyond their undergraduate studies.We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that transcends racial, ethnic, and national boundaries. Regardless of background, our collective goal is to equip our members with the tools, resources, and knowledge needed to thrive in their pursuit of legal education and professional success. In addition, the use of x in womxn is to represent the inclusion and welcoming of all minority womxn, no matter their gender identity. Membership Benefits ❥ Mentorship Program: Connect with established fellow pre-law students, alumni, law professionals, or law students for personalized guidance on law school admissions, career planning, and navigating the legal field. ❥ Networking Opportunities: Attend panels, mixers, and events featuring successful womxn of color in law, offering valuable insights and connections for your legal career. ❥ Academic Support: Join study groups and receive support from fellow pre-law students! ❥ Professional Development: Participate in resume-building sessions, mock interviews, and career workshops to enhance your professional skills and prepare for internships and job opportunities. ❥ Community: Engage in social events, peer support groups, and community service projects that strengthen bonds among members! ❥ Cultural Empowerment: Celebrate and explore the unique experiences of womxn of color in law through discussions, events, and initiatives that highlight diversity and inclusion.

contact: Laura Merchant (she/her/hers) , Email group officers Mission Mission The Office for Residential Education fosters the holistic development of members within the USC residential community. We are committed to providing purposefully inclusive communities that create opportunities for personal and academic success in collaboration with members of the Trojan Family. Vision Our residential communities cultivate and enrich students by integrating learning, engagement and development. We contribute to students’ academic success by offering student-centered programs, services and environments that foster student involvement, responsibility and leadership. Core Values --Community – We value community by developing inclusive living-learning environments that cultivate personal and academic growth and interpersonal relationships. --Inclusion – We value the individual differences of members within our community by creating opportunities for members to embrace and understand the complex backgrounds, ideas, experiences and lifestyles of others. --Social Responsibility – We value one’s responsibility to the common good and shared values of the Trojan Family and challenge students to be mentally conscious and actively working towards creating equality, access and opportunities for others. --Wellness – We value our students’ wellness by encouraging the adoption of behaviors that promote the recognition of physical, psychological, social and spiritual needs necessary to lead balanced and productive lives. --Integrity – We value integrity by instilling a sense of trust and moral character within the USC community. --Scholarship – We value the pursuit of knowledge inside and outside the classroom by encouraging free inquiry and innovation. Our lead-based mentorship structure connects new members with experienced t
contact: Yann Staelens , Peter Morgan , Johnny Sheng , Manuela Londono , Email group officers Mission USC Formula Electric is a student-led engineering team committed to developing the next generation of innovators through the complete design and construction of a high-performance electric race car for the Formula SAE Electric competition. We empower our members to take ownership of every aspect of the vehicle—from welding the chassis and developing software, to sourcing critical components and presenting to industry judges—giving them a holistic engineering experience rooted in collaboration, accountability, and innovation. Membership Benefits USC Formula Electric offers members a direct pipeline into engineering careers through hands-on, industry-relevant project experience. Every member contributes to the full lifecycle of a fully electric Formula-style race car—from initial design and CAD modeling to manufacturing, testing, debugging, and on-track validation. Our lead-based mentorship structure connects new members with experienced teammates, providing guidance in both technical development and career navigation. By working alongside passionate, technically skilled peers to build real-world systems, our members graduate with the kind of experience that stands out in a competitive job market.

contact: Solana Espino , Kayden-Harmony Greenstein Mission SCene Magazine is the University of Southern California's premier campus culture and student experience magazine. Exploring themes that are unique to college students, our editorial is committed to publishing feature stories about identity, selfhood and coming-of-age in the heart of Los Angeles. U set the SCene. We tell your stories. Membership Benefits All active SCene Magazine members are first and foremost entitled to co-ownership of any/all content they create for the purposes of SCene Magazine. This ownership includes the use of SCene content on resumes, portfolios, and job applications, but only extends to the material DIRECTLY assigned to the member by the Executive Board. Furthermore, active members are entitled to respect for their intellectual property, but must be aware that such properties will be reserved for the sole use of SCene Magazine. Additional benefits include becoming an integral part of SCene's passionate community of creatives, who seek to shed light on USC student stories about the intersection of identity and growth on college campuses. This gives the student community a direct line to the media that is published about them, allowing all active SCene members to become more intimately involved in student life.

contact: Benjamin Lindstrom , Email group officers Mission Sigma Phi Delta serves as a premier social-professional fraternity for students pursuing careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). As a values-based organization, we aim to support members in achieving academic excellence, honing professional competencies, and forming meaningful connections. Our membership fosters an environment where members can flourish personally and professionally. We provide opportunities for members to network with fellow STEM students, professors, and industry professionals. This allows members to build strong professional relationships and gain mentorship opportunities. At the core of our organization is a spirit of service, collaboration, and lifelong brotherhood. Our organization unites members around shared goals and values. We promote integrity, responsible citizenship, and commitment to advancing the STEM fields. Members are encouraged to participate in professional development workshops, networking events, community service initiatives, and social activities. These experiences allow members to strengthen communication, teamwork, and leadership abilities. By joining Sigma Phi Delta, members gain a supportive community that motivates them to reach their full potential. Our alumni network contains over 13,000 accomplished professionals who provide mentorship and career assistance. We empower members to work hard in the classroom, develop professionally, play hard with friends, and create unforgettable memories that will last beyond their university years. Membership Benefits At Sigma Phi Delta’s Alpha Chapter at USC, we offer our members more than just a fraternity. We offer them a chance to grow as engineers and as individuals, by providing them with various opportunities and benefits, such as: Exclusive social and brotherhood events, such as hikes, retreats, beach days, and barbecues, where they can bond with their fellow brothers and have fun. Our fraternity is built on the values of friendship, loyalty, and service, and we foster a strong sense of community and belonging among our members. Networking events, professional development, and resume workshops, where they can learn from and connect with our vast alumni network of engineers that spans from 1924 till the present day. Our alumni have worked in diverse fields and industries, and are eager to share their insights and experiences with our members. Leadership opportunities, where they can take on roles and responsibilities within the fraternity and make a positive impact. Our members can run for executive positions, lead committees, organize events, mentor new members, and more. By being involved in the fraternity, our members develop valuable skills and qualities that will help them succeed in their future endeavors. Academic support, where they can receive help and guidance from their brothers on their coursework, projects, and research. Our members have access to our study rooms, library, tutoring sessions, and peer review groups. We also encourage our members to collaborate and innovate with each other on engineering challenges and competitions.

Our commitment extends to Wellness Wednesdays, offering meditation, yoga, sound baths, and relaxation sessions, encouraging holistic well-being. Furthermore, every Tuesday and Thursday, we dedicate 15 minutes "Stop Drop Meditate" to guide students in meditation, enhancing focus and mental clarity in their daily lives. For those seeking a deeper connection and understanding, SKY Campus Happiness organizes Sky Happiness Retreats, providing opportunities to learn breathwork, yoga, and build a sense of community. contact: David Sanchez Mission Opened as a residential college in 2012, the South Residential College includes four traditional-style residence halls: Marks Tower, Pardee Tower, Marks Hall (closed for 2022-2023) and Trojan Hall (closed for 2022-2023). South Residential College is conveniently located near the Doheny & Leavey Libraries, Tutor Campus Center, important campus service departments, several popular classroom buildings, and McCarthy Quad, a great place to relax and enjoy great Los Angeles weather. A signature of this residential area is its close-knit community; many describe South as being a true “family.” The South Residential Colleges are supported by an engaging team of faculty and staff who endeavor to create a vibrant community where emphasis is placed on the transition to college and exploring USC’s First Year Experience programs. Programming provided by faculty and staff emphasizes personal growth and determining one’s best pathway for leadership as a USC student and future graduate. Residents are encouraged to attend Faculty in Residence events and participate in faculty-student interaction outside the classroom. mission: The Student Association for Addiction Science (SAAS) at USC is dedicated to fostering a supportive and collaborative community for undergraduate and graduate students passionate about addiction science. Our mission is to: Educate: Provide comprehensive education and resources about addiction science, including the latest research, treatment strategies, and policy developments. Connect: Facilitate
membership_benefits: Joining SAAS offers a range of exclusive benefits designed to enrich your academic and professional journey in the field of addiction science: Access to Educational Resources: Members receive access to a wealth of educational materials, including the latest research publications, webinars, and workshops that deepen understanding of addiction science. Networking Opportunities: Connect with a divers
contact: Aaryan Midha Mission The Student Association for Addiction Science (SAAS) at USC is dedicated to fostering a supportive and collaborative community for undergraduate and graduate students passionate about addiction science. Our mission is to: Educate: Provide comprehensive education and resources about addiction science, including the latest research, treatment strategies, and policy developments. Connect: Facilitate networking and collaboration among students, faculty, and professionals to create a strong support network and enhance career development opportunities. Advocate: Promote and advocate for equitable policies related to addiction science within the USC community and beyond. Engage: Organize engaging events, workshops, and outreach programs to raise awareness about various forms of addiction, including substance use, technology, gambling, and more. Support: Offer mentorship and professional development opportunities through partnerships with alumni, community leaders, and industry experts. Join SAAS to access relevant training, multidisciplinary networking, advocacy initiatives, and professional development opportunities. Whether you are an undergraduate, graduate student, postdoctoral fellow, faculty member, staff, or alumnus from any discipline, we welcome you to be part of our community. For more information or to get involved, contact us at uscaddictionscience@gmail.com. Follow us on Instagram @usc.saas for news updates, job postings, and mentorship opportunities related to addiction science. Membership Benefits Joining SAAS offers a range of exclusive benefits designed to enrich your academic and professional journey in the field of addiction science: Access to Educational Resources: Members receive access to a wealth of educational materials, including the latest research publications, webinars, and workshops that deepen understanding of addiction science. Networking Opportunities: Connect with a diverse network of students, faculty, alumni, and industry professionals, facilitating collaborations and mentorships that can enhance your career prospects. Professional Development: Participate in skill-building workshops, career panels, and mentorship programs tailored to prepare you for a successful career in addiction science or related fields. Advocacy and Leadership Roles: Take on leadership positions within SAAS, giving you the opportunity to spearhead advocacy initiatives and influence addiction-related policies at USC and beyond. Exclusive Events: Attend members-only events, including guest speaker sessions, roundtable discussions, and social gatherings that provide both educational value and a sense of community. Career Support: Benefit from job postings, internship opportunities, and guidance from professionals in the field, helping you navigate your career path in addiction science. Community Engagement: Engage in community outreach programs that raise awareness about addiction and contribute to meaningful change both on campus and in the broader community.

name: Swim Club at USC
mission: The Swim Club at USC is a student run recreational organization that is dedicated to growing and fostering a community for students interested in the sport of competitive swimming.
membership_benefits: Joining the **USC Swim Club** offers a wide range of benefits that cater to swimmers of all skill levels. Here are some in-depth advantages of becoming a member: 1. Professional Coaching - Experienced Coaches: The club provides access to qualified and experienced coaches who offer personalized training and guidance to help you improve your technique, speed, and overall performance. - Structured Tr
contact: Email group officers Mission The Swim Club at USC is a student run recreational organization that is dedicated to growing and fostering a community for students interested in the sport of competitive swimming. Membership Benefits Joining the **USC Swim Club** offers a wide range of benefits that cater to swimmers of all skill levels. Here are some in-depth advantages of becoming a member: 1. Professional Coaching - Experienced Coaches: The club provides access to qualified and experienced coaches who offer personalized training and guidance to help you improve your technique, speed, and overall performance. - Structured Training Programs: Coaches develop structured and progressive training plans tailored to various skill levels, ensuring consistent improvement and goal achievement. - Technique Improvement: Regular feedback and technique drills help in refining strokes, starts, turns, and finishes, leading to more efficient and effective swimming. 2. Access to Excellent Facilities - State-of-the-Art Pools: Members have access to USC's top-notch aquatic facilities, including Olympic-sized pools equipped for both training and competition. - Training Equipment- Availability of various swimming aids and equipment such as kickboards, pull buoys, and fins to enhance different aspects of your training. - Flexible Practice Times: Multiple practice sessions scheduled throughout the week accommodate different class schedules and personal commitments. 3. Competitive Opportunities - Local and National Meets: The club participates in various competitions, providing opportunities to compete against other universities and clubs, enhancing your competitive experience. - Team Representation: Represent USC at different levels of competition, fostering school pride and personal accomplishment. - Performance Tracking: Regular meets allow you to track your progress, set new personal records, and receive recognition for your achievements. 4. Health and Fitness Benefits - Full-Body Workout: Swimming engages all major muscle groups, improving cardiovascular health, strength, flexibility, and endurance. - Low-Impact Exercise: Ideal for reducing stress on joints while still providing an effective workout, suitable for injury prevention and rehabilitation. - Mental Well-being: Regular swimming is known to reduce stress, improve mood, and boost mental clarity, contributing to overall well-being. 5. Social and Community Engagement - **Like-Minded Peers**: Connect with fellow students who share a passion for swimming, fostering friendships and a supportive community. - Team-Building Activities: Participate in social events, group outings, and team-building exercises that enhance camaraderie and create lasting college memories. - Networking Opportunities: Build connections that can extend beyond college, benefiting future personal and professional endeavors. 6. Leadership and Personal Development - Club Leadership Roles: Opportunities to take on positions such as Team Captain, Event Coordinator, or Treasurer, developing valuable leadership and organizational skills. - Event Planning Experience: Gain experience in organizing meets, social events, and fundraising activities, enhancing project management and teamwork abilities. - Volunteer and Community Service: Engage in community outreach programs and volunteer opportunities facilitated by the club, contributing positively to society and building your resume. 7. Flexible Commitment Levels - Adaptable Participation: Whether you're a competitive swimmer or prefer recreational swimming, the club accommodates various commitment levels and goals. - Inclusive Environment: Welcoming atmosphere for swimmers of all backgrounds and skill levels, encouraging participation and enjoyment of the sport. - Balance with Academics: The club understands academic priorities and offers flexibility to ensure you can balance studies and extracurricular involvement effectively. 8. Enhancement of College Experience - Skill Enhancement: Continuously develop and hone a lifelong skill that promotes a healthy lifestyle. - Resume Building: Active participation and leadership roles in the club can significantly enhance your resume, demonstrating commitment, teamwork, and leadership qualities to future employers.

name: The Mustard Seed Project
mission: The Mustard Seed Project is an innovative volunteer-based service for individuals experiencing homelessness; our mission is to provide low barrier access to basic needs and services for displaced communities around the USC campus & greater LA area. We foster a person-centered approach to homelessness services, and apply best practice and data driven techniques to homeless outreach. We hope to deve
membership_benefits: USC Mustard Seed Project defines membership on three different levels; Open Membership, General/Voting Membership, and Executive Membership. Open membership is not restricted by dues, interviews, or application process – this entails that all members of the USC student body are free to join MSP as an Open Member without being bound by any restrictions. Open Members are able to attend all General M
contact: Chichi Makasi , Madison Palmer , Justin Shih Mission The Mustard Seed Project is an innovative volunteer-based service for individuals experiencing homelessness; our mission is to provide low barrier access to basic needs and services for displaced communities around the USC campus & greater LA area. We foster a person-centered approach to homelessness services, and apply best practice and data driven techniques to homeless outreach. We hope to develop young leaders in professionalism, political competency, and ethics while educating the public, and the USC student body, about homelessness in their respective communities. The Mustard Seed Project, which is affiliated with the International Mustard Seed Project Organization, follows a mission guided by a “hand up, not hand out” philosophy, to connect unsheltered individuals to services and programs through community organization, relationship-based outreach, and volunteer case management. We hope to direct outreach and volunteer projects across Los Angeles to distribute basic supplies such as food and water to displaced populations; we will also work with our Chapter Advisors and the Directors of MSP to disseminate housing, pro-bono consulting, and work opportunities to displaced populations. Membership Benefits USC Mustard Seed Project defines membership on three different levels; Open Membership, General/Voting Membership, and Executive Membership. Open membership is not restricted by dues, interviews, or application process – this entails that all members of the USC student body are free to join MSP as an Open Member without being bound by any restrictions. Open Members are able to attend all General Member meetings, and learn about homelessness in the Greater Los Angeles area. They will also be able to assist in the planning of volunteer events, and partake in two volunteer events per semester. Open Members will be able to learn about the legal, health, and economic implications of displacement in their community, enabling them to educate the rest of the USC student body about the mission of The Mustard Seed Project. General/Voting Members must pay dues of $30.00USD/semester in order to be eligible to vote on matters concerning MSPSC, and have a larger stake in the planning and execution of events. On top of sharing the benefits of Open Membership, these members will also be able to attend more than two volunteer events a semester, and reap the benefits of hands-on and in-field relief with displaced communities in the GTLA area. Executive Membership is limited to the Officers of the Mustard Seed Project; these positions are outlined in the organization's Constitution, and require a formal application and interview process. The details regarding this process can be found in the MSPSC Constitution. These members must still pay the $30.00USD/semester dues required for General Membership, but have the largest stake in planning and executing events. These members are required to attend all General Member meetings, as well as Executive Board meetings, and are responsible for overseeing the wellbeing of the organization.

contact: Yj Si , Maaha Hashmani , Email group officers Mission The mission of The Nucleus is to present the latest in science and technology in an easily digestible manner and to maintain accessibility for all readers. Our students will be the voice of scientific information literacy within the USC student body and prepare and publish media that represents a community driven by a desire to have an impact on global well-being. We encourage our students to find inspiration in everything, and to put together media that ties these elements back to a scientific rational foundation. We will remain dedicated to serving the USC student body by means of credibility, transparency, and community. Membership Benefits Members of The Nucleus will be part of a community of students passionate about improving information literacy within the USC student body. Students will do so as writers, editors, and/or graphic designers publishing articles, infographics, and/or podcasts every 6 weeks. Students who join as writers and editors will have the opportunity to first author their own work in the form of short-form articles. Students who join as graphic designers will be afforded the opportunity to build their portfolio through designs for articles and infographics. Students will also be given the opportunity to be the voice of The Nucleus, through our podcast multimedia team. Students who join weekly meetings will also be provided professional development opportunities, including workshops, volunteering, and discussions with USC faculty and other guest speakers who are experts in the various fields of science, technology, and advocacy.
