contact: Lulu Saldana , Email group officers Mission Trojan Health Interpretation Services' primary mission is to fulfill a demand for interpreters for the Limited English Proficient population in order to guarantee medical services for all. We will provide free health interpretation and translation services for people in and around the USC area. Another primary mission is to train bilingual students to become ad hoc interpreters through extensive medical vocabulary training and practice situations. We seek to provide our services to grassroots organizations that are working with limited resources; usually, these organizations provide free services and do not have the funds or the organizational structure to have a full-time interpreter. Ultimately, Trojan Health Interpretation Services actively works to provide free ad hoc interpretation services to the vast Limited English Proficient population in Los Angeles. Membership Benefits There are many membership benefits from joining Trojan Health Interpretation Services (THIS). First off, THIS members will learn important medical skills and terminology in the target language which they will then be able to utilize in the future. Not only that, but they will become equipped with the tools and skills to interpret because of their interpreter etiquette training. If a member decides to pursue a career path in medicine, these two skills combined could help them become a better health professional and allow them to aid as many people as possible. Furthermore, members will be able to get an inside look at what happens in interactions between patients and physicians while simultaneously positively impacting the local Limited English Proficient community.

contact: Crystal Hsia , Victoria Higgins Mission The USC U.S.-China Institute informs public discussion of the evolving and multidimensional U.S.-China relationship through policy-relevant research, graduate and undergraduate training, and professional development programs for teachers, journalists, and officials. name: Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Program at the University of Southern California
mission: Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) at USC is a volunteer organization sponsored by the IRS, KYCC, Deloitte, and KPMG. VITA is dedicated to serving the Central Los Angeles community through providing free income tax return assistance to residents of the local community. Through training, and by gaining IRS certification, volunteers gain and utilize knowledge of the tax returns and various tax t
membership_benefits: Joining the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program at USC offers a range of valuable benefits. As a VITA volunteer, you'll gain hands-on experience in tax preparation, enhancing your understanding of the U.S. tax system, which is particularly beneficial if you're pursuing a career in accounting, finance, or law. You'll also have the opportunity to develop critical skills such as client com
contact: Email group officers Mission Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) at USC is a volunteer organization sponsored by the IRS, KYCC, Deloitte, and KPMG. VITA is dedicated to serving the Central Los Angeles community through providing free income tax return assistance to residents of the local community. Through training, and by gaining IRS certification, volunteers gain and utilize knowledge of the tax returns and various tax treaties to file income tax returns for community residents. VITA services resident and international students, allowing for a larger range of returns and specialization. Membership Benefits Joining the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program at USC offers a range of valuable benefits. As a VITA volunteer, you'll gain hands-on experience in tax preparation, enhancing your understanding of the U.S. tax system, which is particularly beneficial if you're pursuing a career in accounting, finance, or law. You'll also have the opportunity to develop critical skills such as client communication, problem-solving, and attention to detail, all of which are highly valued by employers. Additionally, VITA allows you to make a meaningful impact on the community by providing free tax assistance to low-income individuals, students, and other underserved groups. This experience not only enriches your resume but also offers a sense of fulfillment from helping others. Furthermore, being involved in VITA connects you with a network of like-minded peers and professionals, opening doors to mentorship, networking opportunities, and potential internships or job placements.

name: WorldMed @ UPC
mission: In 2008, the WorldMed at USC: A Global Health Initiative (WorldMed) was founded by graduates of the inaugural class of the Master of Science in Global Medicine Program. In 2016, WorldMed opened a new chapter at the undergraduate campus (WorldMed @ UPC). The mission of WorldMed @ UPC is to bring together a robust and diverse community of undergraduate, graduate, and medical students, physicians, an
contact: Email group officers Mission In 2008, the WorldMed at USC: A Global Health Initiative (WorldMed) was founded by graduates of the inaugural class of the Master of Science in Global Medicine Program. In 2016, WorldMed opened a new chapter at the undergraduate campus (WorldMed @ UPC). The mission of WorldMed @ UPC is to bring together a robust and diverse community of undergraduate, graduate, and medical students, physicians, and faculty members of the Keck School of Medicine of USC who are eager to understand, discuss, and tackle the critical issues in global medicine. As a response to the pressing need for a comprehensive effort to address global disease, WorldMed @ UPC strives to acquire and disseminate scientific principles related to international medicine and enable its members to take initiatives in combating the global burden of disease in the local community. The organization will also help other nonmembers interested in helping this cause by providing education and resources on how to contribute personally. Membership Benefits
